phase_1.audio: Audio environment is highly distracting with average 63.2 dB exceeding the 60 dB threshold. All 7 confidence-filtered events labeled as 'busy' with 6 Speech and 1 Ambient AI labels, indicating a consistently noisy, conversation-heavy environment unsuitable for focused study.
phase_1.vitals: Heart rate averaged 93.2 bpm, significantly elevated above the calm focus range of 60-85 bpm, suggesting stress or physical activity. Steps averaged 29.8 per sample with 25.3 meters distance per sample, indicating substantial movement rather than seated study behavior.
phase_1.gps_motion: GPS shows drastic location instability with 564.2m displacement and 3,841m total travel distance, far exceeding stable study thresholds. Motion context confirms high activity: 3 walking instances versus only 2 stationary, indicating the user was predominantly mobile during this session.

phase_2.correlations: All modalities strongly correlate toward active movement and environmental distraction. The busy/speech-heavy audio (63.2 dB) aligns with walking motion contexts and substantial GPS travel. Elevated heart rate (93.2 bpm) and continuous step activity (29.8 steps/sample) confirm physical exertion consistent with traveling through noisy environments rather than stationary study.
phase_2.holistic_assessment: This session represents minimal to no actual study concentration. The user was predominantly mobile, traveling nearly 4km through busy, speech-filled environments with elevated physiological indicators of activity. All sensor modalities indicate this was a transit or commute session rather than a focused study period.

phase_3.recommendations: 1) Establish a dedicated, stationary study location to eliminate travel-related distractions and reduce GPS displacement to under 200m. 2) Choose quiet environments with ambient noise below 45 dB and use noise-canceling headphones to block speech distractions. 3) Schedule study sessions separate from commute/travel times when you can remain seated and stationary, allowing heart rate to settle into the 60-85 bpm focus range.

reason: The session shows virtually no concentration indicators, with distracting audio (63.2 dB, all 'busy' labels with speech), elevated heart rate (93.2 bpm), and extensive movement (3,841m travel, 564m displacement). Motion context confirms predominantly walking activity rather than stationary study, indicating this was a commute or transit session rather than focused study time.
